How's it going everyone? In this video we're going to show you the basics of how to set up a Zapier account. There are a couple different ways to do it. If you already have a Google account and you're okay with Zapier using that email account for it, you can use your Google account. If you noticed I'm signed into Google so it gives me the option to just continue right away with that. That's probably the quickest way to create an account. Otherwise, you can go ahead and sign in the email address you want to use. Go ahead and turn your name and go ahead and enter in a good password. Once that's in there, go ahead and click the sign up button there. If you want to save that password you can save it or you can close it out. It's up to you. You can give your company name there. You can choose whichever role fits you best. When you do marketing, you have a few options in here. You don't see what matches you can choose other. Go ahead and hit the continue. Now this is just asking what apps you're going to use the most. You don't have to go through this process but if you know for a fact you are going to end up using Google Sheets and MailChimp and Acuity. You can start adding these in. I'm just typing in here in this box and picking them up as we go. Whenever you add in as many as you want, you are good to go. That's plenty. If you didn't want to do this step, you can just click skip right there or if you want to go and pick common applications like the ones we're using as part of a play, you can choose those. Go ahead and hit finish setup. It's going to take you right to this page. Now by default it's going to give you a 14-day trial. I do recommend upgrading your account so once you get logged into Zapier, look for this notification up top and choose the best plan for you. Now the most important one is probably going to be the professional because it means that your automations are a lot faster. You can scroll down and see the more details between them but if you notice the big difference between the free and the starter, the Zaps only run every 15 minutes. We want Zaps to run every 5 minutes or less if possible and so that's why the professional plan is generally the one we recommend the most. It also lets you have a good number of Zaps and it lets you have Zaps with multiple steps, you have access to pods and some of the more fancy features with it. So whenever you choose which plan, it's your personal preference. I definitely recommend going with a professional hire. That way you won't have any issues implementing the services we use. If you think you're going to use Zapier a lot, you can go ahead and bump up to the professional plan and they also have teams and companies but at the very least I recommend the professional plan. So once you choose the planning one, hit the blue upgrade plan button. It'll give you the option to either pay annually or monthly. Go and turn your billing information and then you upgrade your account. So that's it for our creating your Zap account and also for upgrading to it. I'm going to show you just some of the basics of Zapier so I'm going to hit this explore right there and explore. It lets you look through what apps you can connect. So say you want to make two different things, talk with one another, send data from one to the other, you can start picking them up. You can connect just about anything to anything else. How Zapier works is it gives you a trigger and then it tells it to do something. So if somebody sends you an email with a certain subject or somebody submits a form in Woofoo or somebody creates an appointment in a QT, it can trigger another action and we'll walk you through more in depth once we go through those specific Zaps. This screen right here where it says Zaps. This is where you can actually manage everything. So Zaps are just the individual tasks. If this happens then do that and that's what they mean by Zaps. Folders are a great way to manage your Zaps. So if you use folders for a certain play you can create that folder first. So for instance where it says private folders I'm going to click a plus sign next to that, name a folder there. Once that's typed in I'm going to hit the enter key on my keyboard and now we have a folder to put things in. And once I click on that test I'm going to do make a new Zap and it's going to create it inside of this folder. One really great feature of Zapier is that you're able to see what's happened in the past. So say you have a Zap running and you notice that it just didn't pick something up. It didn't pick up a bit of information. You can click on task history up here and that's going to show you all of the history. So if your Zaps ran 20 times you're going to see that 20 times in this page here and you can click on it for more information. Connected accounts are just the accounts that you've signed in and connected with it and we'll walk you through those steps as you need to. Don't worry about having to worry about connected accounts right now. You can choose to kind of proactively connect them. So for instance if I want to go and connect my Gmail account I can choose this from here. It's going to bring up this page that has me sign in with my Gmail account. That way Zapier is able to use my Gmail account for different things. I'm going to just close that out instead of allowing it. The same goes for any other software that you want to connect. So you can choose WooFoo. It's going to have you sign in with your email address and password. If you have more than one WooFoo account with your email address you have to provide that custom domain and then continue to add it. By connecting your accounts ahead of time it means that you know they're already there but you don't have to because as part of creating zaps and customizing them it gives you that option there too. So Zapier is a really complex program. You can do a whole ton with it. Just kind of think of it as the glue that makes things happen. It makes things talk to each other and it connects applications so that if something happens in one you can have it automatically do something in another. So it's super powerful. I hope you enjoyed this video. I hope you enjoyed the next one. Have a great rest of your day in the meantime.
